cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Celtic languages.
cline: noun. A gradation in a character or phenotype within a species, deme, or other systematic group.
clone: noun. A living organism (originally a plant) produced asexually from a single ancestor, to which it is genetically identical.
clove: noun. A very pungent aromatic spice, the unexpanded flower bud of the clove tree.
coil: noun. Something wound in the form of a helix or spiral.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
colin: noun. The American quail or bobwhite, or related species.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
cove: noun. A hollow in a rock; a cave or cavern.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
elevon: noun. A movable surface at the trailing edge of a tailless airplane (such as the space shuttle) that provides pitch and roll control.
enol: noun. An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group bonded to a carbon atom which is doubly bonded to another carbon atom.
enolic: adjective. Pertaining to, or derived from, an enol.
envoi: noun. A short stanza at the end of a poem, used either to address a person or to comment on the preceding body of the poem.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
eve: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Even.
evince: verb. To show or demonstrate clearly; to manifest.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
lee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lyélé.
lei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemio.
leno: noun. A type of weave used to make light, open cotton fabric used for window curtains.
leone: noun. A unit of currency of Sierra Leone, divided into 100 cents.
lev: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lamma.
levin: noun. Lightning; a bolt of lightning; also, a bright flame or light.
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
line: noun. A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
lion: noun. A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
live: verb. To be alive; to have life.
liven: verb. To cause to be more lively, or to become more lively.
loin: noun. The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips.
love: noun. A deep caring for the existence of another.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
niece: noun. A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother (" fraternal niece "), or of
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
noel: noun. A kind of hymn, or canticle, of mediaeval origin, sung in honor of the birth of Christ; a Christmas carol.
noil: noun. A short fibre left over from combing wool or spinning silk during the preparation of textile yarns.
novel: adjective. Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.
novice: noun. A beginner; one who is not very familiar or experienced in a particular subject.
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.
oleic: adjective. Of or pertaining to oil, especially to vegetable oil.
olein: noun. Any naturally-occurring greasy or oily substance related to fat.
olive: noun. A tree of species Olea europaea cultivated since ancient times in the Mediterranean for its fruit and the oil obtained from it.
once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
oven: noun. A chamber used for baking or heating.
ovine: adjective. Of, being, pertaining to, or resembling a sheep; also, of an animal: from the genus Ovis.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veil: noun. Something hung up or spread out to hide or protect the face, or hide an object from view; usually of gauze, crepe, or similar
vein: noun. A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.
veloce: adverb. (To be played) with great rapidity.
vice: noun. Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
vie: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Vietnamese.
vine: noun. Any plant whose stem requires support and which climbs by tendrils or twining.
viol: noun. A stringed instrument related to the violin family, but held in the lap between the legs like a cello, usually with C-holes, a flat
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
voice: noun. Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special
voile: noun. A light, translucent cotton fabric used for making curtains and dresses.
vole: noun. Any of a large number of species of small rodents of the tribes Arvicolini, Ellobiusini, Clethrionomyini, Pliomyini, Phenacomyini and
